---
title: "Sector Overview"
subtitle: "What the Inland Northwest nonprofit sector looks like today"
---

::: {.key-finding}
<span class="kf-label">Key finding</span>
The Inland Northwest hosts **5,282 active nonprofits**, and its composition
closely mirrors both states' averages — **human services is the largest
classified category (23%)**, ahead of education and public benefit. The sector
is financially bottom-heavy: most organizations report no revenue to the IRS
at all, roughly **three in ten filers bring in under $100,000**, while just 73
organizations exceed $10 million and account for the bulk of the region's
**$6.5 billion** in nonprofit revenue.
:::

{{< include _shared.qmd >}}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
orgs = FileAttachment("data/orgs_active.csv").csv({typed: true})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
subsectors = FileAttachment("data/subsector_counts.csv").csv({typed: true})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
nteeMajor = FileAttachment("data/ntee_major.csv").csv({typed: true})
```

## Composition by category

The NCCS NTEE v2 subsectors group nonprofits into broad fields of activity.
About 30% of registered organizations — mostly small, older, or recently
formed groups — carry no usable NTEE code and are shown as "Unclassified."

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 200,
  marginRight: 70,
  marginBottom: 45,
  height: 420,
  width: 900,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Active organizations", labelAnchor: "center", grid: true, tickFormat: "~s"},
  y: {label: null},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(subsectors.filter(d => d.geo === "Inland Northwest"), {
      x: "n",
      y: "subsector_name",
      sort: {y: "-x"},
      fill: GU.s1,
      rx: 3,
      insetTop: 2,
      insetBottom: 2,
      tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.subsector_name}\n${fnum(d.n)} organizations (${fpct(d.share * 100)} of sector)`
    }),
    Plot.textX(subsectors.filter(d => d.geo === "Inland Northwest"), {
      x: "n",
      y: "subsector_name",
      text: d => fnum(d.n),
      dx: 6,
      textAnchor: "start",
      fill: GU.ink,
      fontSize: 12
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"})
  ]
})
```

::: {.chart-note}
Source: NCCS Business Master File, June 2026 vintage, active organizations in
the seven-county region.
:::

## How the region compares to state averages

Share of each state's (or the region's) active nonprofits by category. The
regional mix tracks the Washington and Idaho statewide profiles remarkably
closely — the Inland Northwest is not under- or over-built in any broad
category relative to its states. The differences that do exist are small:
slightly more religious congregations register here, and slightly fewer arts
organizations than Washington overall.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 200,
  height: 460,
  width: 900,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  marginBottom: 45,
  x: {label: "Share of active nonprofits (%)", labelAnchor: "center", grid: true},
  y: {label: null, domain: subsectors.filter(d => d.geo === "Inland Northwest").sort((a, b) => b.n - a.n).map(d => d.subsector_name)},
  color: {
    legend: true,
    domain: ["Inland Northwest", "Washington (statewide)", "Idaho (statewide)"],
    range: [GU.s1, GU.s2, GU.s3]
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.dot(subsectors, {
      x: d => d.share * 100,
      y: "subsector_name",
      fill: "geo",
      r: 6,
      stroke: "#ffffff",
      strokeWidth: 1.5,
      tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.geo}\n${d.subsector_name}: ${fpct(d.share * 100)} (${fnum(d.n)} orgs)`
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"})
  ]
})
```

::: {.chart-note}
Dots that sit nearly on top of each other mean the region matches the state
profile for that category. Exact values in the tooltip.
:::

## Size distribution

Revenue as most recently reported to the IRS. The majority of registered
nonprofits — small clubs, PTAs, granges, all-volunteer groups — report no
revenue at all (they fall below filing thresholds), so the chart below covers
the 1,551 organizations with a reported revenue figure.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
sizeBuckets = {
  const withRev = orgs.filter(d => d.revenue > 0);
  const buckets = [
    {label: "Under $100k", test: d => d.revenue < 1e5},
    {label: "$100k–$500k", test: d => d.revenue >= 1e5 && d.revenue < 5e5},
    {label: "$500k–$1M", test: d => d.revenue >= 5e5 && d.revenue < 1e6},
    {label: "$1M–$10M", test: d => d.revenue >= 1e6 && d.revenue < 1e7},
    {label: "Over $10M", test: d => d.revenue >= 1e7}
  ];
  return buckets.map(b => ({
    bucket: b.label,
    n: withRev.filter(b.test).length,
    revenue: d3.sum(withRev.filter(b.test), d => d.revenue)
  }));
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  height: 300,
  width: 900,
  marginLeft: 60,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: null, domain: sizeBuckets.map(d => d.bucket)},
  y: {label: "Organizations", grid: true},
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(sizeBuckets, {
      x: "bucket",
      y: "n",
      fill: GU.s1,
      rx: 3,
      insetLeft: 4,
      insetRight: 4,
      tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.bucket}\n${fnum(d.n)} organizations\nCombined revenue: ${fmoney(d.revenue)}`
    }),
    Plot.textY(sizeBuckets, {
      x: "bucket",
      y: "n",
      text: d => fnum(d.n),
      dy: -8,
      fill: GU.ink,
      fontSize: 12
    }),
    Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"})
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  height: 300,
  width: 900,
  marginLeft: 70,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: null, domain: sizeBuckets.map(d => d.bucket)},
  y: {label: "Combined revenue ($)", grid: true, tickFormat: d => d3.format("~s")(d).replace("G", "B")},
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(sizeBuckets, {
      x: "bucket",
      y: "revenue",
      fill: GU.s3,
      rx: 3,
      insetLeft: 4,
      insetRight: 4,
      tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.bucket}\n${fnum(d.n)} organizations\nCombined revenue: ${fmoney(d.revenue)}`
    }),
    Plot.textY(sizeBuckets, {
      x: "bucket",
      y: "revenue",
      text: d => fmoney(d.revenue),
      dy: -8,
      fill: GU.ink,
      fontSize: 12
    }),
    Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"})
  ]
})
```

::: {.chart-note}
The two charts share categories but tell opposite stories: organizations
cluster at the small end, dollars at the large end. The 73 organizations over
$10M — led by Kootenai Health, Gonzaga University, and the region's credit
unions and community health systems — account for the large majority of all
nonprofit revenue in the region.
:::

## Explore every organization

Search and filter all 5,282 active nonprofits. Click column headers to sort.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof tblCounty = Inputs.select(
  ["All counties"].concat(Array.from(new Set(orgs.map(d => d.county))).sort()),
  {label: "County"}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof tblCategory = Inputs.select(
  ["All categories"].concat(Array.from(new Set(orgs.map(d => d.subsector_name))).sort()),
  {label: "Category"}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof tblSearch = Inputs.search(
  orgs.filter(d =>
    (tblCounty === "All counties" || d.county === tblCounty) &&
    (tblCategory === "All categories" || d.subsector_name === tblCategory)
  ),
  {placeholder: "Search names, cities…", columns: ["name", "city"]}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(
  tblSearch.map(d => ({
    Name: d.name,
    City: d.city,
    County: d.county,
    Category: d.subsector_name,
    NTEE: d.ntee ?? "—",
    "Reported revenue": d.revenue > 0 ? fmoney(d.revenue) : "—",
    Since: d.ruling_year ? String(d.ruling_year) : "—"
  })),
  {rows: 15, layout: "auto"}
)
```

::: {.chart-note}
"Since" is the year the IRS granted tax-exempt status. Revenue is the most
recent figure reported to the IRS; "—" means the organization falls below
filing thresholds or has not filed a financial return.
:::
